_comment: Each case lists articles by index. expected_clusters groups those indices. Cases are chosen to probe known failure modes, not just happy paths.

cases.description: Two wires covering the same missile strike within hours — should cluster.

articles.title: Russia fires ballistic missiles at Kyiv, killing at least 8
articles.description: Russian forces launched a wave of ballistic missiles at Kyiv on Monday, striking a residential district and killing at least eight people, Ukrainian officials said.

articles.title: Ukraine capital hit by Russian missile barrage, 8 dead
articles.description: A Russian missile barrage struck Kyiv early Monday, killing eight people and wounding dozens in a residential area, Ukrainian emergency services said.

cases.notes: Strong entity + anchor overlap within 1.5 hours. Should cluster easily.

cases.description: Unrelated events from different regions on the same day — must not cluster.

articles.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile into the sea
articles.description: North Korea launched a ballistic missile that flew over Japan before falling into the sea, South Korea's military said, condemning the test as a provocation.

articles.title: Kenya opposition supporters protest disputed election results
articles.description: Thousands of opposition supporters rallied in Nairobi on Monday to protest what they called a stolen election, with police firing tear gas to disperse crowds.

cases.notes: No shared entities or anchors. Trivially different.

cases.description: Ongoing conflict covered on day 0 and day 5 — same event, but temporal window will split them.

articles.title: Sudan's RSF captures key Khartoum district after fierce fighting
articles.description: Sudan's Rapid Support Forces seized control of a major district in Khartoum on Tuesday after days of intense fighting with the Sudanese Armed Forces, witnesses said.

articles.title: Khartoum fighting intensifies as RSF advances further into city
articles.description: Fighting between the RSF and SAF for control of Khartoum escalated on Sunday, with artillery fire reported across multiple districts, five days after initial clashes.

cases.notes: 120 hours apart. Semantic similarity captures the ongoing conflict meaning despite temporal distance. FIXED by semantic embeddings approach.

cases.description: Two separate protest events in different Egyptian cities — must not cluster.

articles.title: Protesters in Cairo clash with police over economic conditions
articles.description: Thousands of protesters gathered in central Cairo on Wednesday to demonstrate against rising food prices and unemployment, clashing with riot police who deployed tear gas.

articles.title: Anti-government demonstrations erupt in Alexandria
articles.description: Separate demonstrations broke out in Alexandria on Wednesday as Egyptians took to the streets over deteriorating living standards, in what appeared to be uncoordinated protests.

cases.notes: Both mention Egypt, protests, and economic grievances — but city names (Cairo vs Alexandria) and lack of shared anchors keep the score below threshold. Verified correct on first run.

cases.description: A labor strike and a military airstrike — anchor 'strike' should not merge them.

articles.title: French teachers strike over pension reform shuts thousands of schools
articles.description: Hundreds of thousands of French teachers walked off the job on Thursday in a nationwide strike against proposed pension reforms, closing roughly 40 percent of schools.

articles.title: French forces strike militant targets in northern Mali
articles.description: French military aircraft conducted strikes against militant positions in northern Mali on Thursday, killing an estimated 15 fighters, the French defense ministry said.

cases.notes: Semantic scoring now separates labor-action coverage from military-strike coverage even with shared token 'strike' and shared country adjective.

cases.description: Ceasefire announced on day 1, broken on day 2 — these are sequential updates to the same story, not contradictions.

articles.title: Sudan warring factions agree to 72-hour humanitarian ceasefire
articles.description: Sudan's Rapid Support Forces and the Sudanese Armed Forces agreed to a 72-hour ceasefire to allow humanitarian aid into besieged areas, mediators said Saturday.

articles.title: Sudan ceasefire collapses as RSF resumes attacks on Khartoum
articles.description: The brief ceasefire between Sudan's warring factions collapsed on Sunday as the Rapid Support Forces resumed artillery attacks on Khartoum, just hours after it came into effect.

cases.notes: Both are about the same Sudan ceasefire story. Shared entities (Sudan, RSF, Khartoum) and ceasefire anchor keep the score above threshold despite the anchor_conflict penalty. Verified correct on first run.

cases.description: A specific battlefield incident and a macroeconomic consequence of the same war should stay separate events.

articles.title: Israeli strikes hit hospital, homes in south Lebanon
articles.description: Israeli strikes hit residential areas and a hospital in southern Lebanon as cross-border fighting continued.

articles.title: Iran says 30 universities hit in US-Israeli strikes
articles.description: Iranian officials said universities were damaged during US-Israeli strikes amid the wider war.

cases.notes: Regression fixture for the dangerous same-conflict/different-incident pattern. These are related in context but describe different incidents, so they must not merge.

cases.description: A macro analysis/update article and a specific incident-focused report within the same conflict should not merge by default.

articles.title: War on Iran: Three key takeaways from Araghchi's interview with Al Jazeera
articles.description: The piece summarizes diplomatic signals and broader implications from an interview, rather than reporting one concrete battlefield incident.

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 33 of US-Israel attacks?
articles.description: Day-by-day conflict update covering specific attacks and tactical developments reported across active fronts.

cases.notes: Regression fixture for abstraction mismatch: macro takeaways/analysis vs incident-focused operational update. Same conflict context alone must not force a merge.

cases.description: Two strike reports in the same conflict but different theaters should not merge into one event.

articles.title: Houthis claim joint attacks with Iran, Hezbollah targeting Ben Gurion Airport
articles.description: The report focuses on claimed attacks linked to Yemen-based actors and threats around Ben Gurion Airport.

articles.title: Israel claims over 200 strikes on Iran and Hezbollah targets
articles.description: The report summarizes Israeli military operations and strike counts tied to Iran and Lebanon theaters.

cases.notes: Regression fixture for multi-theater strike conflation: shared conflict entities but distinct operational incidents and locations.

cases.description: Two consequence stories tied to the same war but affecting different places and sectors should not merge.

articles.title: India set for searing summer as Iran war strains energy supplies
articles.description: Heat and fuel planning concerns in India follow the conflict-related energy shock.

articles.title: Iran war wipes $120bn off Dubai, Abu Dhabi stock markets
articles.description: UAE financial markets react to the conflict's regional fallout.

cases.notes: Regression fixture for cross-theater consequence spillover: both are war fallout, but one is India energy supply pressure and the other is UAE market impact.
